chief_complaint: Two severe COVID-19 patients, a 45-year-old male and a 62-year-old female, presented with clinical deterioration.
history: This case report analyzes two severe COVID-19 patients without pulmonary embolism, revealing persistently elevated levels of extracellular vesicles (EVs) carrying tissue factor (TF+), complement proteins (C3a+, TCC+), and endothelial markers (CD144+, CD54+).
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings are not provided.
labs: Temporal trends in EV subpopulations correlated with clinical deterioration, suggesting their role in endothelial injury, complement hyperactivation, and thromboinflammation.
correct_diagnosis: High complement expression on extracellular vesicles in Covid-19 patients without pulmonary embolism leading to death
differentials: High complement expression on extracellular vesicles in Covid-19 patients without pulmonary embolism leading to death
correct_ranking: High complement expression on extracellular vesicles in Covid-19 patients without pulmonary embolism leading to death
